aboutText: Gaston Ibarroule is a sound designer and music composer from Neuquén, Argentina, based in Berlin, Germany, since 2015. His career in the world of sound began through music, playing guitar and singing from an early age. Driven by a broad interest in the field, he has developed in different areas including sound design, music composition and production, field recording, dialogue and effects editing and mixing. His body of work spans short and feature-length fiction films, documentaries, experimental films, as well as television, streaming and advertising.
homeIntro: Sound designer & Composer from Argentina, based in Berlin. My work spans film, series, documentary, art installations, TV & ads.

faqs.question: What audio formats does Sonidata support for recording?
faqs.answer: Sonidata records in three quality tiers: Pro (WAV 48 kHz / 24-bit) for professional delivery and post-production, High (WAV 44.1 kHz / 16-bit) for general-purpose capture, and Compressed (M4A AAC) for extended sessions where storage is a priority. Pro quality is available with a Pro subscription.

faqs.question: How does UCS integration work?
faqs.answer: Sonidata fully implements the Universal Category System (UCS v8.2). You assign Category, Subcategory, CatID, FX Name, Creator ID, Source ID, and Location via the Naming View; the app then auto-generates a UCS-compliant filename (e.g., ANMLDog_Bark-Close_GI_MKH8060_Park.wav) and can organize files into a matching folder hierarchy automatically.

faqs.question: Can I control recording with Siri or the Action Button?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sonidata exposes Start Recording, Stop Recording, and Toggle Recording actions to the Shortcuts app. You can trigger them from Siri, Back Tap, the Action Button (iPhone 15 Pro+), or any automation flow. The sonidata:// URL scheme is also available for third-party tools.

faqs.question: What is the Live Activity on my Lock Screen?
faqs.answer: When a recording is running, a Live Activity appears on your Lock Screen and Dynamic Island showing a live timer, the recording name, and a red dot indicator. It starts automatically when you begin recording and disappears when you stop — no configuration needed.

faqs.question: How do FX Name Presets speed up my workflow?
faqs.answer: Presets let you save reusable FX Name templates. Swipe right on the FX Name row to apply a saved template — including a "Last Used" shortcut. Templates support smart placeholders like <Location, State> (auto-filled from GPS) and a Time of Day button that appends the current solar period (Dawn, Morning, Day, Evening, Dusk, Night).

faqs.question: What is the Metadata Clipboard?
faqs.answer: The Metadata Clipboard lets you prepare metadata while a recording is still running. Fill in your fields, tap Copy, then after stopping, open the new file and tap Paste to apply everything in one step. It's ideal for back-to-back field sessions where category and location stay the same.

faqs.question: Does Sonidata embed artwork into audio files?
faqs.answer: Yes. When you attach a photo, it's embedded directly into the audio file — as an ID3v2 APIC tag in WAV files and as iTunes metadata in M4A files. This means the artwork travels with the file and is visible in Finder, Soundminer, AirDrop previews, and other tools without needing a separate image.

faqs.question: Is the CSV export compatible with Soundminer?
faqs.answer: The CSV headers are aligned with Soundminer V5 field names — including FXName, Designer, Manufacturer, Microphone, CategoryFull, Channels, and more — so you can import directly into Soundminer without manual column remapping.

faqs.question: Can I import audio from Voice Memos or other apps?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Use the iOS Share button in any app (Files, Voice Memos, etc.) and select Sonidata. The file is copied to a shared container and automatically moved into your Documents folder the next time you open the app — ready for UCS tagging.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Free and Pro?
faqs.answer: Free users can record in High and Compressed quality, tag files with UCS metadata, use SmartSearch, FX Name Presets, Batch Delete, Siri Shortcuts, and the Live Activity. Pro unlocks WAV 48/24 recording, Voice Slate, Smart Location, Metadata Clipboard, Photo Attachments, Embedded Artwork, Folder Browser, Calendar Filter, Map Viewer, Batch Rename, Visual Date Export, and ZIP Export. Pro is a one-time lifetime purchase — no subscription.

faqs.question: What is Sonidata Embed?
faqs.answer: Sonidata Embed is a free desktop companion app for macOS. It takes the ZIP exports from your iOS device and automatically embeds the metadata (BEXT, iXML, CUE markers, and artwork) directly into your original recorder's WAV files so they can be read natively by Soundminer, Pro Tools, and Reaper.

content.text: Sonidata records high-quality audio directly on your iPhone or iPad. Choose from three quality presets to match your session requirements — from broadcast-standard WAV to lightweight compressed capture.
Audio Quality Options
To change quality: Settings → Recording → Audio Quality. Pro quality (WAV 48/24) is a Pro feature. Free users can record in High and Compressed quality.
How to Record

items: Tap the center record button (red circle) on the bottom tab bar.
A recording panel appears at the bottom with a live waveform, timer, and format badge.
Tap the stop button to finish recording.
The Naming View automatically opens so you can tag the recording immediately.

content.text: Recording Indicator
While recording, the record button pulses with a red glow on the Naming and Settings tabs — a subtle visual reminder that a session is active even when you navigate away from the recording screen.
Live Activity (Lock Screen)

When a recording is in progress, a Live Activity appears on your Lock Screen and Dynamic Island showing a live timer with the current duration, the recording name, and a red dot indicator. It starts automatically when you begin recording and disappears when you stop — no setup required.
Record Ready / Monitor Mode

Optional pre-record monitoring that shows live audio levels before recording starts. Uses an input tap for zero-file metering. The gradient background (green→yellow→red) on the monitor panel acts as a level meter, supporting VU and Peak ballistics in Settings. The center button offers a 3-state flow (idle→monitor→record→stop).
Post-Recording Discard

When you finish recording, two pill buttons ("Discard Recording" / "Continue Editing") appear at the top of the Naming View. Discard safely deletes the file, removes the database entry, and dismisses the sheet instantly.
Recording Tips

The app records in the background — you can switch to other apps without stopping the session. The waveform display shows real-time audio levels (30 bars) so you can monitor your input visually. New recordings appear in your library instantly after stopping — no need to wait for a filesystem scan. You can rename them immediately using the Naming View, or tag them later from the file list.
Files are saved to the app's Documents folder with a default name like "New Recording 2026-02-28_14-30-00.wav". Use the Naming View to apply proper UCS metadata right away.

The Naming View is the core of Sonidata. It's where you assign UCS-compliant metadata to your recordings using the industry-standard Universal Category System (UCS v8.2) — ensuring your files are named consistently and compatible with any sound library manager.
UCS Filename Format
{CatID}{UserCategory}_{VendorCategory}{FXName}_{CreatorID}_{SourceID}_{Location-UserData}
Example: ANMLDog_Bark-Close_GI_MKH8060_Park.wav
Metadata Fields
How to Tag a Recording

items: Search for a category using SmartSearch — type "dog" and it finds ANIMALS > DOG even if you don't know the exact name.
Select from the dual-wheel picker (Category on left, Subcategory on right).
Fill in the FX Name, or use Voice Slate to dictate it.
Add Creator ID and Source ID — enable "Remember IDs" in Settings to save across sessions.
Add Location — tap the location button to auto-fill from GPS, or type manually.
Attach a Photo if desired (tap the camera icon at the top).
Preview the filename in the bottom bar, then tap "Rename File".

content.text: Auto Title Case
An optional setting in Settings → Naming automatically capitalizes the first letter of each word in the FX Name field. This works seamlessly with live typing, pasting metadata, or using Voice Slate.
FX Name Presets

Speed up your naming workflow with reusable FX Name templates. Swipe right on the FX Name row to see your saved presets, including a "Last Used" shortcut. Create and manage custom templates in Settings → FX Name Presets. Templates support smart placeholders — use <Location, State> and it will be auto-filled with your actual GPS location when applied.

Tap the ☀️ button to append the current solar period (Dawn, Morning, Day, Evening, Dusk, Night) to your FX Name — localized in all 21 supported languages. In Voice Memos mode, recordings are auto-named using your GPS location (e.g., "Berlin 001.wav") so nothing stays unnamed.
SmartSearch

The category search uses Apple's Natural Language framework (NLEmbedding) for semantic understanding — not just text matching. Type "rain" to find WEATHER > RAIN and related categories. Synonym matching means "puppy" finds ANIMALS > DOG. Exact category matches score highest (×20), followed by subcategory, synonyms, explanations, and semantic neighbors.
Metadata Clipboard (Copy / Paste)

Prepare metadata while a recording is still running and apply it to the finished file in one tap. During recording, fill in Category, FX Name, Creator ID, and any other fields, then tap Copy. After stopping, open the Naming View for the new file and tap Paste — all fields are populated instantly. The Paste button only appears when there is metadata in the clipboard.
The Metadata Clipboard is ideal for back-to-back field sessions where the category and location stay the same between takes.
Batch Rename

Rename multiple files at once: enter Edit Mode, select files, tap "Rename". The Naming View opens in batch mode. All selected files receive the same metadata and are renamed sequentially with unique indices to keep filenames distinct.
Batch Delete

Quickly remove multiple recordings at once. Enter Edit Mode and select the files you want to remove, then tap the red trash button in the batch action bar. Confirm in the alert and all selected files are moved to trash. Batch delete works across All Files, Folder Contents, and the Record View — and it's free for all users.
Voice Slate lets you dictate the FX Name using speech-to-text instead of typing — ideal when your hands are occupied with gear or you're working quickly between takes.
How to Use

items: In the Naming View, tap the microphone icon next to the FX Name field.
Speak clearly — describe the sound (e.g., "Bark Close").
The transcription appears in the FX Name field.

content.text: Technical Details

Uses Apple's native SFSpeechRecognizer — all processing happens on-device, completely private. Sonidata injects dynamic contextual hints from UCS categories to improve transcription accuracy for sound design terminology. Audio is recorded to a temporary PCM 16 kHz mono file, then transcribed.
Voice Slate Language
You can change the recognition language in Settings → Recording → Voice Slate Language. Options include Device Default and all languages supported by Apple's Speech framework.
Voice Slate is free for all users.
Control Sonidata hands-free with Siri, the Shortcuts app, Back Tap, or the Action Button. Three dedicated actions let you manage recording sessions without ever touching the screen.
Available Actions

rows: Starts or stops depending on current state

content.text: How to Set Up

items: Open the Shortcuts app on your iPhone.
Create a new shortcut and search for "Sonidata".
Choose an action (Start, Stop, or Toggle).
Assign the shortcut to a trigger: Siri voice command, Back Tap (double or triple tap the back of your phone), the Action Button (iPhone 15 Pro and later), or a home screen widget.

content.text: Deep Linking
Sonidata also supports the sonidata:// URL scheme for integration with third-party automation tools and custom workflows.
Assign "Toggle Recording" to Back Tap for instant one-handed recording — no need to unlock or open the app.
Sonidata can automatically tag recordings with GPS location data, embedding geographic context directly into your file metadata.
How It Works

items: Grant location permission when prompted (or enable it in iPhone Settings → Privacy → Location).
When you open the Naming View, the current location is automatically detected.
Tap the location button to auto-fill the Location field.
The full address, coordinates (latitude/longitude), and short address are all saved with the recording.

content.text: Location Accuracy

With "When In Use" permission, location is fetched each time you open the app. With "Always" permission, location is cached and refreshed every 5 minutes in the background — so metadata is ready instantly when you tag. Reverse geocoding converts GPS coordinates to a human-readable address.
What Gets Stored
Enable "Always" location access for the fastest metadata loading. Sonidata pre-fetches your location as soon as the app becomes active.
Sonidata offers four ways to browse your recordings — each designed to help you find the right file quickly, whether you're searching by name, folder, date, or location.
All Files (List View)

A flat list of every recording in your library. Tap any file to expand its row and see the location, attached photo, and an inline audio player with waveform. When a Vendor Category is set, it's displayed directly on the file row as a badge for quick reference. Search by filename, sort by name, date, or category, and use filter pills to narrow by category, subcategory, or location. Swipe left for Edit / Share / Delete, swipe right to Select.
Folder Browser
A grid view of your UCS folders showing folder name and file count. Tap a folder to see its contents, with the same swipe actions and expandable rows as the list view.
Calendar Filter Bar

Filter your folder grid by recording date using the horizontal filter bar at the top of the Folder Browser. Tap a Year pill to narrow the view, then drill into a Month, then a specific Day. The folder grid and file counts update in real-time to show only matching recordings — a fast way to locate files from a specific session.
Map Viewer

An interactive map showing all geotagged recordings. Each pin represents a recording with GPS coordinates. Filter by category or location, and tap a pin for inline playback. The Map Viewer stays loaded in the background so switching back is instant — no reload delay.
Switch between views using the segmented control at the top of the screen. Your preference is saved between sessions.
Sonidata can automatically organize your recordings into a UCS folder structure — so your files mirror the same hierarchy used by professional sound libraries.
Organization Modes

rows: All files stay in Documents root

content.text: How to Configure

Go to Settings → Folder Organization. Enable Auto-Organize to move files to their UCS folder immediately after renaming. Use "Generate Folders" to create the complete UCS folder tree in your Documents directory (based on all categories in the UCS database), or "Organize All" to batch-organize existing recordings based on their current metadata.
Attach a photo to any recording — useful for documenting the recording environment, location, source, or any visual reference you want linked to the audio file.
How to Attach

items: In the Naming View, tap the camera icon at the top of the screen.
Choose "Take Photo" or "Choose from Library" from the compact dropdown menu.
The photo is saved to Documents/Photos/ with a unique ID linking it to the recording.

content.text: Photos are automatically compressed to save storage while preserving quality. They are included in ZIP exports alongside the audio files.
Embedded Artwork

Attached photos are automatically embedded into your audio files. WAV files receive the artwork as an ID3v2 APIC tag; M4A files receive it as iTunes metadata. This means your artwork travels with the audio file — visible in Finder, Soundminer, AirDrop previews, and other tools without needing a separate image file.
Photo Attachments and Embedded Artwork are Pro features.
CSV Export

Export your entire recordings database as a CSV file for import into sound library managers. CSV headers are aligned with Soundminer V5 field names — including FXName, Designer, Manufacturer, Microphone, CategoryFull, Channels, and more — for seamless import without manual column remapping. Also includes all UCS metadata fields, location data, coordinates, file paths, audio format, file size, duration, sample rate, and bit depth.
Access via Settings → Export → Export CSV.
ZIP Export

items: Open the Export Filter Sheet from Settings or the file browser.
Set filters: category, subcategory, location, creator ID, date range.
Preview how many files match.
Export as ZIP: includes audio files + photos + CSV metadata.
Share via AirDrop, Files, email, or any share destination.

content.text: Visual Date Export

Export recordings organized by date. In the Export Filter Sheet, use the calendar date picker to select specific Year → Month → Day ranges. Enable "Group by Date" to create date-named subfolders inside the ZIP (e.g., 2026-03-05/). Each subfolder contains only the recordings from that day, keeping your archive neatly organized by session date.
Library Statistics
The Export screen shows your library stats at a glance: total recordings, categories used, and file counts.
ZIP Export and Visual Date Export are Pro features.
Import external audio files into Sonidata using the iOS Share Extension — bring in recordings from other apps and tag them with proper UCS metadata.
Supported Formats
.wav, .m4a, .mp3, .aiff, .aif, .caf
How to Import

items: In any app (Files, Voice Memos, etc.), tap Share.
Select Sonidata from the share sheet.
The file is copied to Sonidata's shared container.
Next time you open Sonidata, the file is automatically moved to your Documents folder.
It appears in your recordings list, ready for tagging.

content.text: Voice Memos files are fully supported — share directly from the Voice Memos app into Sonidata for UCS tagging.
Sonidata offers a generous free tier for everyday recording and tagging, with a Pro upgrade that unlocks the full professional toolkit. Pro is a one-time lifetime purchase — no subscription.
Feature Comparison
Pro Lifetime is a one-time purchase — no recurring fees, no subscription.
Sonidata is designed with a strict on-device privacy model. Your recordings, metadata, and personal data never leave your device unless you explicitly export or share them.
Data Handling

items: All data stays on your device. Sonidata does not upload recordings or metadata to any server.
Voice Slate uses Apple's on-device speech recognition — audio never leaves your device.
Location data is stored locally and embedded in file metadata. It is not shared with anyone.
Cloud backups go to your personal iCloud / Google Drive account — Sonidata has no access to your cloud storage.

content.text: No analytics, no tracking, no ads. Your sound library is yours alone.
Sonidata's interface is localized in 21 languages. UCS category names are translated from the official UCS v8.2.1 translations database.

English, French, German, Spanish, Portuguese (Portugal), Portuguese (Brazil), Chinese (Simplified), Chinese (Traditional), Japanese, Korean, Arabic, Russian, Ukrainian, Italian, Dutch, Turkish, Polish, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, Finnish.
Recordings don't appear in the list

Recordings now appear instantly after stopping. If one is missing, re-open the app to trigger a filesystem rescan. Check that the recording is in the app's Documents folder (not a system folder). If the file was moved manually via the Files app, Sonidata will re-sync on next launch.
Voice Slate isn't working

Check that Speech Recognition permission is granted: iPhone Settings → Sonidata → Speech Recognition. Ensure you have a stable internet connection for the first download of the speech model (subsequent uses are offline). Try changing the Voice Slate Language in Settings if accuracy is poor.
Location isn't auto-filling

Check permission: iPhone Settings → Sonidata → Location → "Always" (recommended) or "While Using". If using "While Using", the location may take a moment to fetch when you first open the Naming View. GPS accuracy is lower indoors — step outside for a more precise reading.
Files aren't organizing into folders

Check that Folder Organization is enabled: Settings → Folder Organization → Mode (set to Category or Full). Ensure Auto-Organize is turned on. If files were recorded before enabling organization, use "Organize All" to batch-process them.
Siri Shortcuts don't trigger recording

Make sure Sonidata has been opened at least once after the update so the App Intents are registered with the system. Check Settings → Siri & Search and confirm Sonidata shortcuts are visible. If using Back Tap, verify it's configured: Settings → Accessibility → Touch → Back Tap.
Live Activity not showing on Lock Screen
Live Activities must be enabled: iPhone Settings → Sonidata → Live Activities. Ensure you're running iOS 16.1 or later (required for ActivityKit).
Export ZIP is empty

Check that your filters actually match some recordings (the preview count shows how many files will be included). Make sure the recordings have audio files on disk (missing files are shown with a "missing" indicator).
Importing from Voice Memos doesn't work
Make sure you're using the Share button in Voice Memos, not drag-and-drop. If the import seems stuck, close and re-open Sonidata — the file will be picked up automatically.
Theme looks wrong or colors are stuck
Switch themes in Settings → Display → Theme. If using System, the app follows your iPhone's appearance setting (Settings → Display & Brightness).
Sonidata Embed is a desktop companion app for macOS (Windows coming soon) that takes your Sonidata session exports and embeds the metadata directly into your recorder's WAV files.

After embedding, your WAV files carry all their metadata — UCS categories, GPS coordinates, markers, artwork — as industry-standard chunks that Soundminer, Pro Tools, Reaper, and other tools can read natively.
The Workflow

items: Export a session from Sonidata iOS as a ZIP file (Settings → Export → Export ZIP).
Open Sonidata Embed on your Mac and drag-and-drop the ZIP file.
Drop your WAV folder — the app matches recorder filenames from the CSV to your WAVs.
Review metadata, waveforms, markers, and photos in the detail sidebar.
Click "Embed All" — metadata is written into the WAV files.
Open in your DAW — Soundminer, Reaper, Nuendo, or Pro Tools will read the embedded data.

content.text: What Gets Embedded

rows: FX Name, Creator ID, Source ID, timecode
Full UCS metadata, GPS, location, markers
Marker positions with labels and comments
Standard RIFF tags (INAM, IART, ICMT, etc.)

content.text: Unlocking Pro Features

Sonidata Embed is free to download, but unlocking full export capability requires a Sonidata Pro license from the iOS app. We use an Offline Pairing Code to verify your purchase, ensuring 100% privacy without requiring logins or cloud accounts.

items: Open Sonidata on your iPhone/iPad.
Navigate to Settings → Desktop App Unlock.
If you have purchased Sonidata Pro, the app will display a secure, 6-digit offline pairing code.
Open Sonidata Embed on your Mac/PC and click Unlock Pro.
Enter the pairing code. Your desktop app is now permanently unlocked.

content.text: Note: During the open beta phase, the app may be temporarily unlocked for all users for testing purposes. If not in beta, this code is required to embed metadata into files.

Sonidata's workflow takes you from raw field recordings to a fully tagged, metadata-embedded, UCS-organized sound library — without modifying your original files. Whether you record with the built-in iPhone microphone or an external recorder, the result is the same: DAW-ready WAV files with industry-standard metadata that any sound library manager can read.
This guide walks through the entire pipeline in four phases. Estimated time: 10–15 minutes per session after initial setup.
Phase 1 — In the Field (Sonidata iOS)
This is where it all starts. You're on location, capturing sounds. Sonidata supports two recording paths:

rows: Record directly with iPhone mic (Mono, Dual Mono, True Stereo, or M/S)
Quick captures, ambient recordings, solo sessions
Create a metadata session alongside your dedicated recorder (Zoom, Sound Devices, etc.)

content.text: Built-in recording captures the audio and creates the database entry simultaneously. External recording creates only the metadata session — you'll match it to the recorder's WAV files later on your Mac.
Naming & Tagging

items: Search for a UCS category — SmartSearch finds categories semantically, so "puppy" → ANIMALS > DOG.
Add FX Name — describe the sound (e.g., "Bark-Close"). Use Voice Slate to dictate if your hands are occupied.
Set Creator ID & Source ID — your initials and microphone. Enable "Remember IDs" so these persist.
Attach a photo — optional but valuable. Photos are embedded directly into the WAV file as artwork.
Tap Rename — the file is renamed to UCS-compliant format (e.g., ANMLDog_Bark-Close_GI_MKH8060.wav).

content.text: Use Metadata Clipboard (Copy/Paste) to tag multiple recordings with the same category in seconds.
Automatic Metadata

Sonidata automatically captures GPS coordinates (latitude/longitude, reverse-geocoded to a readable address), altitude, date & time, and full audio format details (sample rate, bit depth, channels, duration).
Markers (External Recorder Only)
When using the External Recorder, tap Add Marker during recording to log timecoded cue points. These are embedded as CUE/ADTL chunks in your WAV files.
Export

When your session is complete, export it. Built-in recordings: Settings → Export → Export ZIP. The ZIP contains your WAV files, photos, and a session CSV. External recorder sessions: Tap Export Session in the External Recorder view. The ZIP contains only the CSV and photos — your audio files are on the recorder's SD card.
Phase 2 — Transfer to Mac
Built-in Recordings (AirDrop / ZIP)

items: AirDrop the exported ZIP directly to your Mac, or save to Files and transfer via cable / iCloud Drive.
Drop the ZIP into Sonidata Embed — it automatically detects the Sonidata session.
The app extracts the CSV and photos, triggers the metadata scan, and you're ready to match WAV files immediately.

content.text: External Recorder (SD Card / SSD)

items: Eject the SD card from your recorder and mount it on your Mac (or copy files from an external SSD).
In Sonidata Embed, drop the CSV or ZIP from the iOS export.
Drop the WAV folder from your recorder.
Sonidata Embed matches recorder filenames from the CSV (e.g., ZOOM0001_TrLR.wav) to your actual WAV files.

content.text: Filename matching is exact — don't rename your recorder's WAV files before importing.
Phase 3 — Embed & Organize (Sonidata Embed for macOS)
This is where your metadata meets your audio files.
Non-Destructive by Default

Sonidata Embed never modifies your original files. When you click "Embed All", it creates a duplicate folder next to your originals with the _sonidata suffix, copies your WAV files into the new folder, writes metadata into the copies, renames files to UCS-compliant filenames, and generates a soundminer_import.csv with 17 columns ready for Soundminer import. Your original folder stays exactly as it was.
What Gets Embedded

rows: FX Name, Creator ID, Source ID, timecode reference
Full UCS categories, GPS coordinates, location, markers, project/scene/take
Marker positions with labels and comments
Standard RIFF tags (INAM, IART, ICMT, IGNR, ISRC, IPRD, IKEY)

content.text: All non-standard chunks (SMED, _PMX) already present in your WAV files are preserved.
Library Organizer
After embedding, use the Library Organizer to sort your files into a UCS-compliant folder structure on your studio drive.

items: Select your library drive — the destination for organized files.
Choose UCS folder mode — files are sorted into Category/Subcategory/ folders automatically.
Preview first — see exactly where each file will go before committing.
Copy — files are copied (not moved) to your drive with full folder hierarchy.

content.text: The organizer includes duplicate detection (name + size matching), import history (.sonidata_imports.json log), and auto folder creation for missing categories.
Phase 4 — Library Ready

At this point your files are: renamed to UCS-compliant filenames, embedded with iXML, BEXT, markers, and artwork, organized into category/subcategory folders on your studio drive, and accompanied by a Soundminer-compatible CSV for bulk import.
Import into Your Sound Library Manager

Open your library manager (Soundminer, BaseHead, Soundly, etc.) and either scan the folder — the embedded metadata is read directly from the WAV files — or import the CSV (soundminer_import.csv) containing all 17 metadata columns for one-click bulk import.
DAW Compatibility
Embedded artwork is visible in Finder, sound library managers, and AirDrop previews — DAWs generally don't display artwork.
Flow — Built-in Recording

Record with iPhone → Name & Tag → Auto Metadata (GPS) → Export ZIP
  ↓
AirDrop ZIP to Mac
  ↓
Sonidata Embed auto-detects ZIP → Embed All → Organize → Library Ready
Pros: Single device, all audio + metadata in one ZIP, fastest path from field to library. Cons: Limited to iPhone microphone quality and stereo modes.
Flow — External Recorder

Create Session → Live Metadata Tap → Add Markers → Name & Tag → Export Session
  ↓
Transfer CSV/ZIP to Mac + Mount SD card / SSD with WAV files
  ↓
Sonidata Embed matches CSV → WAV folder → Embed All → Organize → Library Ready
Pros: Use professional microphones and recorders (Zoom F6, Sound Devices MixPre, etc.), multi-channel capture, higher sample rates. Cons: Two-device workflow, requires filename matching.
Both Paths Converge

Regardless of which recording path you choose, the output is identical: session.csv with all metadata in a structured format, photos/ with embedded artwork for each recording, and WAV files — either from the iPhone (internal) or your recorder (external). Sonidata Embed treats both paths the same way.
Quick Reference

rows: iXML, BEXT, CUE/ADTL, LIST-INFO, ID3 Artwork

content.text: Requirements

rows: Required for ZIP Export, Photo Attachments, Stereo Recording
Sonidata Pro license + Offline Pairing Code

content.text: Tips for Faster Sessions

items: Set up Creator ID and Source ID once — enable "Remember IDs" in Settings so they persist between sessions.
Use FX Name Presets — save common naming patterns (e.g., <Location, State> Ambience) and apply them with one swipe.
Copy/Paste metadata — tag the first file, copy the metadata, and paste it onto subsequent files. Only change the FX Name.
Batch rename — select multiple files in the library and tag them all at once with sequential numbering.
Use Voice Slate — dictate FX Names hands-free while recording on location.
Keep the same folder structure — use the Library Organizer's UCS folder mode consistently so your studio drive stays organized as your library grows.
